layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN LAYOUT with equal-width side-by-side tables spanning the full available slide space below the title. The slide uses a 4-column × 6-row grid structure with 3% safe-area margins on all sides and 2% horizontal gutter between the two table containers. Both tables are aligned at the top edge and share identical height, filling the vertical space from below the title to the bottom safe-area margin. The layout follows a left-to-right reading flow with the Bookings table on the left and the Revenues table on the right. No outer content container is used — the tables sit directly on the slide background. No decorative bottom layer is needed as the two tables provide full coverage of the content zone. The design applies Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) from the design guidelines: no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey label cells for region/customer identifiers, and thin dashed grey separator lines between data rows. The visual treatment is formal and regulatory-appropriate, matching the financial data content register. This iteration addresses the judge's critical feedback by ensuring correct row structure parsing: multi-line customer names (e.g., 'U Chicago Thundercat (VA)', 'InterMountain (IM)', 'New US - 2026', 'New CA - 2026') are each treated as single table rows, not split across multiple rows. All customer rows from the source are included without omission.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.46, 0.54]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Bookings Table): Left-aligned table occupying the left half of the slide (split 1:1 with C1). Top edge aligns with C1 at approximately 13.5% from slide top. Height extends to fill remaining vertical space down to the bottom safe-area margin. Width is approximately 39% of slide width. Content: multi-column financial table showing Bookings data for 2026 by Region, Customer, Country, and quarterly breakdown (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar). The table contains the following rows in order: (1) Header row with column labels: Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar. (2) NA region section: 'U Chicago' (US, 1100, 1100, -, -), 'McGill' (CA, 630, 630, -, -), 'WashU' (US, 150, 150, -, -), 'JGH' (CA, 150, 150, -, -), 'New US - 2026' (US, 45, -, -, 45), 'New CA - 2026' (CA, 150, -, -, 150), 'Total NA' (2225, 2030, -, 195). (3) Intl region section: 'Assuta' (IL, 186, 186, -, -), 'Ichilov' (IL, 65, 65, -, -), 'Clalit' (IL, 25, 25, -, -), 'Sheba' (IL, 115, 115, -, -), 'Albert Einstein' (Intl, 850, -, 850, -), 'New Intl - 2026' (DACH, 691, -, -, 691), 'Total Intl' (1932, 391, 850, 691). (4) Grand total row: 'Total' (4157, 2421, 850, 886). CRITICAL: Each customer name listed above must occupy exactly ONE row. Multi-word customer names (e.g., 'New US - 2026', 'New CA - 2026', 'Albert Einstein', 'New Intl - 2026') are single entries, not split across multiple rows. Visual treatment follows Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table): Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns (not a single full-width line). The first column (Region) uses light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of row height) with bold brand dark text left-aligned with a small left inset — these are the region label cells (NA, Intl). The Region cell spans multiple data rows vertically (merged cell effect) covering all customers within that region. Customer and Country columns use plain text on white background, left-aligned. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) use plain text on white, right-aligned for numeric values. Empty cells (represented by '-' in source) are rendered as blank cells with no text. Data rows are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space below the header structure. Between each data row: thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines spanning the full table width. Subtotal rows (Total NA, Total Intl) and grand total row (Total) use bold text to distinguish them from data rows. Highlighted cells (visible green fills in source for specific Jan/Feb/Mar values) preserve their exact highlight color as accent fills behind the cell text. All text uses the theme body font. Column headers are bold, slightly larger or same size as body. Data text is regular weight, standard body size. Font size is reduced proportionally to fit all rows within the available container height without overflow — target range is medium body size, ensuring all numeric values and text labels fit comfortably within their cells. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column narrow (single word 'NA' or 'Intl'); Customer column sized to fit longest customer name ('New Intl - 2026') without wrapping; Country column narrow (2-4 character country codes); numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining width equally and are narrow enough to feel compact but wide enough to display 4-digit numbers comfortably with right alignment.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Revenues Table): Right-aligned table occupying the right half of the slide (split 1:1 with C0). Top edge aligns exactly with C0 at approximately 13.5% from slide top. Height matches C0 exactly, extending to the bottom safe-area margin. Width is approximately 46% of slide width. Content: multi-column financial table showing Revenues data for 2026 by Region, Customer, Country, and quarterly breakdown (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar). The table contains the following rows in order: (1) Header row with column labels: Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar. (2) NA region section: 'U Chicago' (US, 976, 949, 14, 14), 'Thundercat (VA)' (US, 176, 59, 59, 59), 'InterMountain (IM)' (US, 45, 15, 15, 15), 'WashU' (US, 133, 129, 2, 2), 'JGH' (CA, 133, 129, 2, 2), 'McGill' (CA, 557, 6, -, 543, 8), 'Total NA' (2020, 1287, 635, 99). (3) Intl region section: 'Manchester' (UK, 293, 98, 98, 98), 'Netcare' (Intl, 28, 9, 9, 9), 'Albert Einstein' (Intl, 781, -, -, 781), 'Hadassah' (IL, 372, 366, 3, 3), 'Assuta' (IL, 119, 104, 7, 7), 'Ichilov' (IL, 18, 6, 6, 6), 'Clalit' (IL, 16, 5, 5, 5), 'Sheba' (IL, 118, 104, 7, 7), 'Total Intl' (1744, -, 104, -). (4) Grand total row: 'Total' (3765, 1979, 770, -). CRITICAL: Each customer name listed above must occupy exactly ONE row. Multi-word or parenthetical customer names (e.g., 'U Chicago', 'Thundercat (VA)', 'InterMountain (IM)', 'Albert Einstein') are single entries, not split across multiple rows. The source slide shows 'U Chicago' and 'Thundercat (VA)' as two separate customer rows under NA; 'InterMountain (IM)' as a third separate row. SVGSketcher must parse these as distinct single-row entries. The 'Sheba' row (IL, 118, 104, 7, 7) must be included in the Intl section — it was missing in the previous attempt. Visual treatment follows Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table), identical to C0: Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns. The first column (Region) uses light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of row height) with bold brand dark text left-aligned with a small left inset. The Region cell spans multiple data rows vertically (merged cell effect) covering all customers within that region. Customer and Country columns use plain text on white background, left-aligned. Numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) use plain text on white, right-aligned for numeric values. Empty cells (represented by '-' in source) are rendered as blank cells with no text. Data rows are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space below the header structure. Between each data row: thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines spanning the full table width. Subtotal rows (Total NA, Total Intl) and grand total row (Total) use bold text. Highlighted cells (visible green fills in source for specific numeric values) preserve their exact highlight color as accent fills behind the cell text. All text uses the theme body font. Column headers are bold, slightly larger or same size as body. Data text is regular weight, standard body size. Font size matches C0 exactly — same reduction applied to maintain within-slide uniformity (Rule C6). Target range is medium body size, ensuring all numeric values and text labels fit comfortably within their cells without overflow. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column narrow (single word 'NA' or 'Intl'); Customer column sized to fit longest customer name ('InterMountain (IM)' or 'Thundercat (VA)') without wrapping; Country column narrow (2-4 character country codes); numeric columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining width equally and are narrow enough to feel compact but wide enough to display 4-digit numbers comfortably with right alignment. The right edge of this table respects the 3% right safe-area margin.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BOTTOM LAYER — Inactive): Not active. The two tables (C0 and C1) provide full coverage of the content zone with no significant empty space remaining. No visual grouping benefit would be added by a bottom decorative layer, and the formal financial data register does not call for additional background fills. The slide background remains white.
  SC-TOP (TOP CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): Not active. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed. The two tables are independent data presentations with no visual links between them.
